members.name: GetDateDictFromSystem
members.summary: Returns the current date as a dictionary of keys: [code]year[/code], [code]month[/code], [code]day[/code], and [code]weekday[/code]. The returned values are in the system's local time when [param utc] is [code]false[/code], otherwise they are in UTC.

members.name: GetDateDictFromUnixTime
members.summary: Converts the given Unix timestamp to a dictionary of keys: [code]year[/code], [code]month[/code], [code]day[/code], and [code]weekday[/code].

members.name: GetDateStringFromSystem
members.summary: Returns the current date as an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD). The returned values are in the system's local time when [param utc] is [code]false[/code], otherwise they are in UTC.

members.name: GetDateStringFromUnixTime
members.summary: Converts the given Unix timestamp to an ISO 8601 date string (YYYY-MM-DD).

members.name: GetDatetimeDictFromDatetimeString
members.signature: public Dictionary GetDatetimeDictFromDatetimeString(String datetime, bool weekday)
members.summary: Converts the given ISO 8601 date and time string (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS) to a dictionary of keys: [code]year[/code], [code]month[/code], [code]day[/code], [code skip-lint]weekday[/code], [code]hour[/code], [code]minute[/code], and [code]second[/code]. If [param weekday] is [code]false[/code], then the [code skip-lint]weekday[/code] entry is excluded (the calculation is relatively expensive). [b]Note:[/b] Any decimal fraction in the time string will be ignored silently.

members.name: GetDatetimeDictFromSystem
members.summary: Returns the current date as a dictionary of keys: [code]year[/code], [code]month[/code], [code]day[/code], [code]weekday[/code], [code]hour[/code], [code]minute[/code], [code]second[/code], and [code]dst[/code] (Daylight Savings Time).

members.name: GetDatetimeDictFromUnixTime
members.summary: Converts the given Unix timestamp to a dictionary of keys: [code]year[/code], [code]month[/code], [code]day[/code], [code]weekday[/code], [code]hour[/code], [code]minute[/code], and [code]second[/code]. The returned Dictionary's values will be the same as the [method get_datetime_dict_from_system] if the Unix timestamp is the current time, with the exception of Daylight Savings Time as it cannot be determined from the epoch.

members.name: GetDatetimeStringFromDatetimeDict
members.summary: Converts the given dictionary of keys to an ISO 8601 date and time string (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). The given dictionary can be populated with the following keys: [code]year[/code], [code]month[/code], [code]day[/code], [code]hour[/code], [code]minute[/code], and [code]second[/code]. Any other entries (including [code]dst[/code]) are ignored. If the dictionary is empty, [code]0[/code] is returned. If some keys are omitted, they default to the equivalent values for the Unix epoch timestamp 0 (1970-01-01 at 00:00:00). If [param use_space] is [code]true[/code], the date and time bits are separated by an empty space character instead of the letter T.

members.name: GetDatetimeStringFromSystem
members.signature: public String GetDatetimeStringFromSystem(bool utc, bool useSpace)
members.summary: Returns the current date and time as an ISO 8601 date and time string (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). The returned values are in the system's local time when [param utc] is [code]false[/code], otherwise they are in UTC. If [param use_space] is [code]true[/code], the date and time bits are separated by an empty space character instead of the letter T.

members.name: GetDatetimeStringFromUnixTime
members.signature: public String GetDatetimeStringFromUnixTime(int unixTimeVal, bool useSpace)
members.summary: Converts the given Unix timestamp to an ISO 8601 date and time string (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS). If [param use_space] is [code]true[/code], the date and time bits are separated by an empty space character instead of the letter T.

members.name: GetOffsetStringFromOffsetMinutes
members.summary: Converts the given timezone offset in minutes to a timezone offset string. For example, -480 returns "-08:00", 345 returns "+05:45", and 0 returns "+00:00".

members.name: GetTicksMsec
members.summary: Returns the amount of time passed in milliseconds since the engine started. Will always be positive or 0 and uses a 64-bit value (it will wrap after roughly 500 million years).

members.name: GetTicksUsec
members.summary: Returns the amount of time passed in microseconds since the engine started. Will always be positive or 0 and uses a 64-bit value (it will wrap after roughly half a million years).

members.name: GetTimeDictFromSystem
members.summary: Returns the current time as a dictionary of keys: [code]hour[/code], [code]minute[/code], and [code]second[/code]. The returned values are in the system's local time when [param utc] is [code]false[/code], otherwise they are in UTC.

members.name: GetTimeDictFromUnixTime
members.summary: Converts the given time to a dictionary of keys: [code]hour[/code], [code]minute[/code], and [code]second[/code].

members.name: GetTimeStringFromSystem
members.summary: Returns the current time as an ISO 8601 time string (HH:MM:SS). The returned values are in the system's local time when [param utc] is [code]false[/code], otherwise they are in UTC.

members.name: GetTimeStringFromUnixTime
members.summary: Converts the given Unix timestamp to an ISO 8601 time string (HH:MM:SS).

members.name: GetTimeZoneFromSystem
members.summary: Returns the current time zone as a dictionary of keys: [code]bias[/code] and [code]name[/code]. - [code]bias[/code] is the offset from UTC in minutes, since not all time zones are multiples of an hour from UTC. - [code]name[/code] is the localized name of the time zone, according to the OS locale settings of the current user.

members.name: GetUnixTimeFromDatetimeDict
members.summary: Converts a dictionary of time values to a Unix timestamp. The given dictionary can be populated with the following keys: [code]year[/code], [code]month[/code], [code]day[/code], [code]hour[/code], [code]minute[/code], and [code]second[/code]. Any other entries (including [code]dst[/code]) are ignored. If the dictionary is empty, [code]0[/code] is returned. If some keys are omitted, they default to the equivalent values for the Unix epoch timestamp 0 (1970-01-01 at 00:00:00). You can pass the output from [method get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time] directly into this function and get the same as what was put in. [b]Note:[/b] Unix timestamps are often in UTC. This method does not do any timezone conversion, so the timestamp will be in the same timezone as the given datetime dictionary.

members.name: GetUnixTimeFromDatetimeString
members.summary: Converts the given ISO 8601 date and/or time string to a Unix timestamp. The string can contain a date only, a time only, or both. [b]Note:[/b] Unix timestamps are often in UTC. This method does not do any timezone conversion, so the timestamp will be in the same timezone as the given datetime string. [b]Note:[/b] Any decimal fraction in the time string will be ignored silently.

members.name: GetUnixTimeFromSystem
members.summary: Returns the current Unix timestamp in seconds based on the system time in UTC. This method is implemented by the operating system and always returns the time in UTC. The Unix timestamp is the number of seconds passed since 1970-01-01 at 00:00:00, the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Unix_time]Unix epoch[/url]. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike other methods that use integer timestamps, this method returns the timestamp as a [float] for sub-second precision.
